site_goal: Serve as a curated editorial reference database for gem collectors, jewelry designers, and gemology students, providing authoritative classification, grading, and visual reference across all major gem species
description: A jewel-toned dark-luxury editorial reference site presenting gemological knowledge as a collector's archive. Deep navy and charcoal page backgrounds contrast with saturated gemstone accent colors — emerald, sapphire, ruby, amethyst, citrine — applied per gem family. Classification cards fill dense grid layouts, each card subtly keyed to its gem's color family. Navigation follows a curated collection model with a persistent side rail and breadcrumb trails through eight detailed reference sections.
motif: jewel-toned dark luxury editorial — deep navy and charcoal backgrounds (#1c1c2e base) with saturated gemstone accent colors shifting by gem family, gold rule dividers, serif specimen labels, and editorial pull-quote typography

pages.layout_pattern: full-width hero with featured specimen spotlight + horizontal curated card strip + editorial gem-of-the-week panel
sections: hero featured specimen with large photograph and classification badge
gem of the week editorial spotlight
quick-navigation taxonomy grid by mineral class

pages.layout_pattern: filterable 4-column classification card grid with left sidebar facet filters by mineral system, hardness, crystal structure, and gem family
sections: filter sidebar with collapsible facet groups

pages.layout_pattern: tabbed reference panels per gem type with full-width comparison charts, visual grading scales, and certification equivalency matrix
sections: clarity grading chart with inclusion diagrams

pages.layout_pattern: masonry gallery grid with slide-in detail drawer showing SVG facet diagrams, proportions, and recommended gem types
sections: masonry gallery grid of cut illustrations
facet count and angle reference table
historical development and regional origin notes

pages.layout_pattern: full-bleed interactive SVG world map with clickable deposit markers and collapsible regional detail panel on the right
sections: world map canvas with deposit point markers
deposit type and gem family legend
regional detail panel with mine profiles

pages.layout_pattern: alphabetical sticky index rail on left with scrollable definition entries, inline cross-reference links, and search input at top
sections: definition entry list with gem family tags

pages.layout_pattern: structured guide pages with multi-column lab comparison table, lab profile cards, and treatment disclosure reference
sections: certification overview and why it matters
major lab comparison table (GIA, AGL, Gübelin, SSEF)
how to read a grading report guide
treatment disclosure standards by gem type

pages.layout_pattern: editorial long-form single-column with pull quotes, contributor bios, and sourcing methodology notes

message_intent: establish authoritative expertise in gemological reference
make complex classification data visually accessible and browsable
evoke the luxury and rarity of fine gemstones through UI aesthetic
support both casual collector browsing and deep gemologist research
content_model: GemSpecimen: name, species, variety, trade names, hardness, crystal system, refractive index, specific gravity, treatment status, origin regions, images
CutStyle: name, category, facet count, crown angle, pavilion angle, SVG diagram, best gem types, historical notes
GradingScale: gem type, grading authority, color descriptor scale, clarity grade map, cut proportion ranges
GlossaryTerm: term, definition, etymology, gem families, related term links
OriginDeposit: country, region, coordinates, gem types, geological period, deposit type, notable mines
interactions: facet filter selection and reset on mineral index sidebar
gem type tab switching on grading page
deposit marker click revealing regional detail panel on origins map
cut gallery masonry card opening detail drawer
hover jewel-tone color-shift on gem classification cards

animations.description: On mouseenter, the gem classification card's CSS background-color transitions from deep charcoal (#1c1c2e) to a rich jewel tone keyed to the card's data-gem-family attribute — #0d3d2e for beryl, #0e1f4a for blue corundum, #3d0e1a for red corundum, #2a0e4a for amethyst, #3d2e00 for chrysoberyl — while border-color transitions from #3a3a4a to the gem's saturated accent (#2ecc71, #3498db, #e74c3c, #9b59b6, #f1c40f respectively); timing is ease-in-out over 600ms; on mouseleave all colors return to base values over the same duration; card position, size, and page layout remain entirely unchanged throughout

asset_needs: SVG world map of major gem deposit locations with coordinate data
SVG facet diagram illustrations for 20+ cut styles
high-resolution mineral specimen photographs per gem species
grading scale gradient graphics for color and clarity
gem family color palette design tokens
mobile_behavior: single-column stacked layout; bottom-sheet filter drawer replaces sidebar; origins map switches to searchable regional list; masonry gallery becomes 2-column
